topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The plastic stick felt heavy in my palm, the stark lines of the results blurring under the harsh fluorescent light of the clinic waiting room. My breath hitched, a shallow, quick intake of air that did little to steady the tremor in my hands. I kept glancing at the clock on the wall, the seconds stretching into an agonizing eternity. When the final confirmation flashed on the screen—a match, undeniable and absolute—a strange, hollow sensation settled in my chest. I wanted to look away, to sink into the patterned carpet, but my feet felt rooted to the spot. "So," the counselor began, her voice unnervingly calm, "this confirms the biological link." I managed a small, tight nod, my throat suddenly constricted. Every muscle in my jaw felt locked, refusing to relax.

[story 2]

I stared at the screen, the data swimming before my eyes. It was a cascade of genetic markers, and suddenly, everything felt incredibly fragile. My stomach churned, a cold knot tightening low in my abdomen. I fiddled incessantly with the edge of my sleeve, tracing the worn fabric with my thumb as if the texture could somehow anchor me to reality. When the official report was read aloud, the sound seemed muffled, distant. A wave of disorientation washed over me, making the room tilt slightly. I gripped my bag strap so hard my knuckles turned white, fighting the urge to bolt, to simply disappear into the anonymity of the hallway. My gaze kept darting towards the door, anticipating an exit that didn't seem to exist.

[story 3]

The silence in the small office was oppressive, thick with unspoken implications. I sat rigidly on the edge of the visitor's chair, my posture painfully straight. My palms were slick with a sudden, clammy sweat, and I could feel the heat rising up my neck. When the technician presented the printout, I didn't reach for it immediately; instead, I focused intently on a tiny smudge on the desk, trying to anchor my attention somewhere small and manageable. When the words confirmed the connection, a sharp intake of breath escaped me. I swallowed hard, the movement catching in my dry throat. I looked up, my eyes wide and unblinking, searching the other person’s face for some reassurance that wasn't there.
